reports.greeting: The Sioux Falls market stayed steady this week with 726 pending sales flat from last week and a list to sale ratio of 97.6 percent. That's the kind of consistency that lets us plan accordingly and capitalize on opportunities without chasing trends. Healthcare expansion continues driving employment growth in our market, which means strong buyer demand for Amy Stockberger Real Estate.

marketStats.headline: Sioux Falls market stats this week
bullets: Median sale price: $340,750. average days on market: 68. list to sale ratio: 97.6%.
Pending count is flat versus last week. Steady week.

items.title: Food & Drink
items.summary: New restaurants and food concepts shape Sioux Falls dining options. Our agents talk to clients about neighborhood amenities constantly.

items.title: Health Care
items.summary: Healthcare expansion drives employment and population growth in our market. Strong job markets mean strong buyer demand for Amy Stockberger Real Estate.

items.title: Development
items.summary: New projects reshape Sioux Falls neighborhoods and commercial corridors. Our team tracks development activity to advise clients on emerging opportunities.

mortgage.headline: Rates and mortgage chatter
mortgage.summary: Inflation data swings mortgage rates like a pendulum, and agents who ignore purchase application trends get left behind. The market rewards those who stay sharp on economic signals and adjust their strategy accordingly. You need to understand what drives rates and how buyers respond to those shifts if you want to compete in today's market. Amy Stockberger Real Estate watches these metrics religiously because your commission depends on it. Stop guessing about what's coming next and start reading the data.
keyPoints: Inflation directly impacts mortgage rates and buyer purchasing power
Purchase applications reflect real market demand and buyer confidence
Agents who track economic indicators outperform those who don't

Stop leaving money on the table when rates shift.

reports.greeting: Welcome to the week of May 4. We've got 726 pending sales in Sioux Falls and a 98.0% list to sale ratio, which tells us the market is moving inventory efficiently. Stay sharp on the fundamentals: understand your local labor market and track mortgage rates like you track your listings because that knowledge closes deals.

bullets: Median sale price: $325,000. Average days on market: 67. List to sale ratio: 99.2%.
Pending count is flat versus last week. Steady week.

items.title: Sanford Health to merge with Minnesota's North Memorial Health
items.summary: Sanford expands into the Twin Cities market through a new merger with North Memorial Health. Watch for downstream effects on Sioux Falls jobs, hiring patterns, and the housing demand that follows a major employer's growth.

items.title: Spring Parade of Homes features standout barndominium
items.summary: A luxury barndominium hits the spring Parade of Homes tour. Worth a drive-through if you have clients chasing unique-property dreams or entertaining space outside the standard build.

items.title: Upscale Mexican restaurant opens at Lake Lorraine
items.summary: A full-service Mexican restaurant lands at Lake Lorraine on the west side. Useful talk track for clients eyeing west-side neighborhoods or anyone you want to take to a new spot.

mortgage.headline: Rates and mortgage chatter
mortgage.summary: Logan Mohtashami breaks down the current labor data and mortgage rates to show agents what's actually driving housing demand right now. The jobs report matters because it signals whether buyers have the income and confidence to make moves. Agents who track employment trends and mortgage rate shifts gain an edge in their market. You win deals by understanding economic fundamentals, not by guessing what happens next. Know your local labor market and rate environment like you know your inventory. That knowledge converts leads into closed transactions.
keyPoints: Labor data directly impacts buyer purchasing power and market demand
Mortgage rates and employment trends shape housing activity
Agents who understand economic fundamentals outperform competitors

items.title: 3 Instagram updates agents need to know about
items.summary: Instagram changed how reach works in 2026. The Broke Agent breaks down the algorithm shift and the new discovery feed so you adapt before your reach drops.

items.title: What today's youngest homebuyers want from agents
items.summary: NAR's 2026 Generational Trends Report shows Gen Z makes up 4 percent of buyers but already changes how homes get bought. Read it before you treat your next Gen Z lead like a 35-year-old buyer.

items.title: 10-minute AI workflow that turns old listing photos into new content
items.summary: Krys Benyamein walks through a fast workflow using Higgsfield and ChatGPT to turn old listing photos into cinematic sunset reels. New content without a re-shoot.

spotlight.name: NewEdge Advisors
spotlight.pitch: Spring is when homeowners need to get serious about their financial strategy. NewEdge Advisors helps you align your home investments with your long-term wealth goals. Work with them to build a plan that actually protects your assets and sets you up for success.

asreLab.headline: ASRE Lab
asreLab.tipHeadline: Send Mother's Day texts from a smarter starting point
asreLab.tip: Pick five past clients you have not connected with in 90 days. Feed Claude their name, the month they closed with you, and one detail you remember. Ask for a no-sales Mother's Day text in your voice. Send all five before tonight. Reply rates on personal-touch outreach run 3 to 5 times higher than mass send. Watch what comes back.
